data.label: Render (PDF → page PNGs, Python)
data.description: Rasterizes the uploaded document to per-page PNG images so the vision steps get raster pages, NOT a raw PDF (Ollama rejects a PDF in `images[].path` with HTTP 500 `image: unknown format`). Generic 'rasterize document to page images' stage — no clinic-domain vocabulary. Uses PyMuPDF (`pymupdf`/`fitz`) at 150 dpi. Reads the upstream file-ref `start.document_file` (Envelope borrow — metadata, NOT bytes; the step fetches the bytes from the file-ref url/key) and emits `page_1` (kind: file, the lead-page vision-borrow target), plus `pages` (all page file-refs) and `page_count`.

data.label: Classify (vision LLM, raw file)
data.description: Vision-only classification on the RAW uploaded file — runs BEFORE OCR (Decision 3: classify→ocr ordering). The model looks at the document image directly (no OCR text available yet) and returns one document_type plus a confidence. NEEDS-LIVE-EXPERIMENTATION: classifying without OCR text is unvalidated — a vision model may misread dense lab tables vs. narrative letters without the text layer. Only explicit lab classes (lab_result / laborergebnis) route into the bloodwork branch; everything else (including befund) falls through to the generic branch.

config.system_prompt: You are a document classification assistant. Look at the document image and classify it into exactly one of the allowed classes, with a confidence in 0..1. You have NO OCR text — judge from the visual layout alone.
config.prompt:
Classify this document into ONE of: lab_result, laborergebnis, befund, referral_letter, imaging_report, discharge_summary, consultation_note, insurance_form, invoice, consent_form, other.

Return JSON: {document_type, confidence}.

data.label: Route by class
data.description: DAG branch on classify.document_type. Only explicit lab classes route to the bloodwork (OCR + bbox-grounded) branch; befund is NOT pinned to bloodwork in Phase 1 — it (and every other class) falls through to the generic extractor.

data.label: OCR (Surya, per-word boxes)
data.description: Bloodwork-branch OCR. Surya emits the full text plus per-word bounding boxes (normalised 0..1) with a global word_index and page — the geometry the downstream resolve-bbox step unions into per-field visual_refs. No model field: Surya IS the OCR engine.

data.label: Extract — Bloodwork (vision + OCR text)
data.description: Lab-result + metadata extractor fed BOTH the page image (render.page_1, for visual layout/structure) AND the OCR text rows (ocr_layout, for exact characters). Emits patient/lab metadata (category patient_info/lab_info) for cross-checking plus every analyte row (category test_result: key=exact test name, value, unit, reference_range). Reasoning is disabled (medgemma is non-reasoning; reasoning models loop here). Bounding boxes are NOT emitted — resolve-bbox grounds each field via Surya word geometry matched by key.

config.system_prompt:
You are a medical lab-report extractor. You are given the page IMAGE plus the same report as OCR text rows (one row per line; columns left-to-right: test-name, value, unit, reference-range). Use the image for layout and structure and the OCR text for exact characters; cross-check them. EVERY field you output MUST have a `category`. Extract two kinds of fields:
(1) DOCUMENT METADATA (category 'patient_info' or 'lab_info') for patient cross-checking and report provenance — use these stable English keys (value = the text as printed; omit any the document lacks): patient_name, patient_date_of_birth, patient_sex, patient_id, lab_name, report_date, lab_number.
(2) LAB RESULTS (category 'test_result'), one per analyte row — `key` = the test name EXACTLY as spelled (it is matched back to the document layout downstream); `value` = the patient's measured number; `unit` = the unit; `reference_range` = the normal-range column. `reference_range` is REQUIRED on every field: for a test_result set it to the printed normal-range column (e.g. <35, 0.-129, 28-100, 0.50-1.20, 60-100 — present on essentially every analyte row); for metadata fields (patient_info/lab_info) set it to the empty string "". value and reference_range are DIFFERENT columns — never copy the range into value, and never leave reference_range empty when the row shows a range.
config.prompt:
Extract the patient/report metadata AND every lab value from this lab report. The OCR rows below mirror the page image (analyte rows: test-name, value, unit, reference-range, left-to-right).

OCR rows:
---
{{ ocr_layout.layout_text }}
---

Return JSON conforming to the schema: {fields: [{key, value, unit, reference_range, confidence, category}]}.

data.label: OCR layout (Python)
data.description: Reconstructs column-aligned table rows from Surya's per-word geometry (group words by row-y, order each row left-to-right by x), so the extractor reads aligned 'test-name value unit reference-range' lines instead of Surya's flattened reading order (which interleaves columns and causes shifted rows / range-as-value errors). Reads ocr.words (read-arc borrow).

data.label: Resolve bbox (Python)
data.description: Grounds each extracted field to a visual_ref {page (1-based), bbox{x,y,w,h ∈ 0..1}} using Surya's OCR word geometry DIRECTLY — never the LLM. Matches the field's test-name key to its OCR row (value-match fallback) and unions that row's word boxes. Reads ocr.words (read-arc borrow) + extract_bloodwork.fields.

data.label: Extract — Generic (vision)
data.description: Generic-fallback branch for every non-lab class (including befund). No OCR layer and no bbox grounding — the vision model reads the raw file directly and emits a flat fields[] list.

config.system_prompt: You are a general-purpose document extractor. Pull every identifiable structured or semi-structured field from the document image. EVERY field MUST have a `category`: 'patient_info' for patient identity, 'lab_info' for issuing-lab/report provenance, 'test_result' for a measured result with a value, otherwise 'other'.
config.prompt:
Extract every identifiable field from this {{ classify.document_type }} document.

Return JSON conforming to the schema: {fields: [{key, value, unit, reference_range, confidence, category}]}.

data.label: Merge branches
data.description: Bloodwork (resolve-bbox) and generic (extract-generic) branch tails converge here. `mode: "any"` — XOR-join, fires on the first incoming control token (the dual of route-by-class's decision split). Re-parks whichever branch's payload under slug `extraction` so downstream validate does a single clean borrow extraction.fields.

data.label: Validate (shape coerce, Python)
data.description: Shape-coerces the merged fields into a defaults-filled list (preserving any visual_ref the bloodwork branch produced) and stamps the document_type from the classifier. Reads extraction.fields + classify.document_type.
